title: Building with Palantir AIP: Logic Tools for RAG/OAG - Palantir Blog

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: A decision-centric extension of RAG that grounds LLMs in an enterprise's operational reality via the Ontology, unifying data, logic, and actions in a single system.

extractedEntities.name: Retrieval Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: Technique enabling LLMs to leverage and cite context-specific outside sources to reduce hallucinations and promote trust in generated responses.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Tool
extractedEntities.description: An OAG tool that enables LLMs to invoke deterministic functions such as forecasting models, optimizers, and business rules alongside non-deterministic LLM reasoning.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Reusable logic encapsulated in the Ontology that can be invoked by LLMs as tools, encompassing machine learning, forecasting, optimization, physical models, and business rules.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform that combines LLM capabilities with the Ontology to build end-to-end enterprise workflows.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: A no-code development environment within AIP for building LLM-powered functions using Ontology-driven tools for data, logic, and action.

extractedEntities.name: Solution Designer
extractedEntities.description: A visual tool in AIP for designing and displaying the overall structure of AI-powered solutions and their tool interconnections.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: A core Ontology concept representing a category of real-world entities (e.g., customers, distribution centers, orders) with typed properties.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology concept defining operations that modify object data, such as updating customer orders or reassigning inventory.

extractedEntities.name: ModelOps Lifecycle
extractedEntities.description: The complete model operations lifecycle in AIP spanning problem definition, candidate development, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and iteration.

extractedEntities.name: Supply Chain Recovery
extractedEntities.description: A Titan Industries use case where AIP surfaces impacted orders after a distribution center fire, identifies alternative centers, and updates orders to prevent shortages.

extractedEntities.name: Prophet Forecasting Model
extractedEntities.description: A Meta-developed regressive forecasting framework used as a logic tool to predict customer orders from historical Ontology data.

keyInsights: OAG extends RAG by grounding LLMs in the three pillars of enterprise decision-making: data, logic, and actions, all unified through the Ontology.
Logic tools bridge the gap between LLM strengths (contextual reasoning) and traditional computation (forecasting, linear optimization) by letting LLMs invoke deterministic functions.
The ModelOps lifecycle in AIP covers the full span from problem definition through deployment and monitoring, supporting both in-platform and externally-built models.
Enterprise logic assets (forecasting models, optimizers, business rules) can be wrapped as Ontology functions and made available to LLMs without sacrificing trust or determinism.
The supply chain use case demonstrates how OAG logic tools combine historical Ontology data retrieval with external forecasting (Prophet) to support operational recovery decisions.

title: Reducing Hallucinations with the Ontology in Palantir AIP - Palantir Blog

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: AIP's approach to hallucination reduction that retrieves structured Ontology objects and their connections to keep LLM reasoning narrow, accurate, and grounded in operational reality.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element representing structured entities that provide grounded context to LLMs, reducing the risk of hallucination by supplying factual data.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Typed attributes of Object Types that supply specific, structured data points to LLMs as context, constraining responses to factual information.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationship between Object Types that allows LLMs to traverse connected data, enabling richer contextual grounding.

extractedEntities.name: Object View
extractedEntities.description: A curated view of an Object Type that controls which properties and links are visible to LLMs, limiting context to only what is necessary for a task.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that reduces hallucinations by giving LLMs the right structured context from the Ontology from the start, rather than correcting after the fact.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology logic that can be surfaced as a tool for LLMs, enabling them to perform structured operations on grounded data.

extractedEntities.name: Retrieval Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: A foundational technique for providing LLMs with external context, which OAG extends by using structured Ontology objects rather than unstructured documents.

extractedEntities.name: Next Token Prediction
extractedEntities.description: The fundamental LLM mechanism that predicts statistically likely continuations without requiring truthfulness, which is the root cause of hallucinations.

extractedEntities.name: Context Window Grounding
extractedEntities.description: The strategy of focusing on what LLMs are suited for (text generation) while supplying structured, relevant context to constrain outputs to factual information.

keyInsights: Hallucinations stem from the fundamental nature of LLMs as next-token predictors: they generate statistically plausible text without requiring truthfulness.
AIP's approach is preventive rather than corrective -- it gives LLMs the right structured context from the start via the Ontology, rather than trying to fix hallucinations after they occur.
OAG provides structured objects with typed properties and relationships, which is more constraining and reliable than traditional RAG's unstructured document retrieval.
Object Views control which properties and links are visible to LLMs, implementing the principle of least privilege for AI context to minimize hallucination risk.
The key insight is to focus on what LLMs are actually suited for (generating text from context) while ensuring the context itself is grounded in structured, verified Ontology data.

title: How Palantir AIP Improves LLM Explainability - Palantir Blog

extractedEntities.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
extractedEntities.description: An LLM prompting strategy that guides models to produce intermediate reasoning steps, improving transparency and enabling users to trace how conclusions are reached.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Tool
extractedEntities.description: Deterministic, purpose-built tools that LLMs delegate specific tasks to, creating composable and auditable AI systems where each component is interpretable.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that improves explainability by decomposing AI workflows into transparent, traceable components through tool delegation and orchestration logging.

extractedEntities.name: LLM Debugger
extractedEntities.description: An AIP tool for tracking the full orchestration trace of LLM interactions, enabling developers to inspect each step, tool call, and decision in an AI workflow.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology-backed logic that serves as an interpretable building block in AI systems, whose inputs and outputs can be independently inspected and verified.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Structured Ontology entities that provide transparent, verifiable data context for LLM reasoning, contributing to system-level explainability.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology operations whose execution can be traced and audited as discrete steps in an AI workflow, supporting explainability.

extractedEntities.name: Tool Delegation
extractedEntities.description: An architectural pattern where LLMs delegate specific tasks to purpose-built, interpretable tools rather than processing everything internally, creating composable and auditable systems.

extractedEntities.name: Orchestration Trace
extractedEntities.description: A detailed log of every step in an LLM-powered workflow including tool calls, inputs, outputs, and reasoning, viewable through the LLM Debugger.

extractedEntities.name: Explainable AI
extractedEntities.description: A subfield of AI focused on building interpretable models and understanding the cause of predictions, applied at the system level through transparent component composition.

keyInsights: AIP achieves explainability at the system level by decomposing AI workflows into transparent, traceable components rather than trying to explain individual model internals.
Tool delegation is a key pattern: LLMs invoke deterministic, purpose-built tools for specific tasks, creating composable systems where each component can be independently inspected.
The LLM Debugger provides full orchestration traces showing every tool call, input, output, and reasoning step, making the entire AI workflow auditable.
Chain-of-Thought prompting complements tool delegation by making LLM intermediate reasoning visible, but system-level transparency is more important than individual model explainability.
Explainability goes hand-in-hand with transparency -- understanding the AI system's constituent parts and their interactions is more practical than fully understanding any single model.

title: AIP Logic - Overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: A no-code development environment for building, testing, and releasing LLM-powered functions that leverage the Ontology through an intuitive block-based interface.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Function
extractedEntities.description: An LLM-powered function built in AIP Logic that takes inputs (Ontology objects or text strings) and returns outputs (objects, strings) or makes edits to the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Block
extractedEntities.description: A composable unit in AIP Logic functions that performs a specific operation: Ontology read/write, calculations, aggregations, or LLM interactions, chained together to form complete functions.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology concept representing reusable logic that can be authored via AIP Logic's no-code interface or through code workspaces.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that serve as inputs and outputs for Logic functions, providing structured data context for LLM-powered operations.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology operations that Logic functions can trigger to make edits, which can be automatically applied or staged for human review.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Typed attributes of Object Types that serve as specific data inputs within Logic function blocks.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that AIP Logic is part of, providing the broader framework for AI-powered enterprise workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Human-in-the-Loop Review
extractedEntities.description: A pattern where Logic function Ontology edits can be staged for human review before being applied, ensuring oversight in automated workflows.

keyInsights: AIP Logic democratizes AI function development by providing a no-code block-based interface, removing the complexity of development environments and API calls.
Logic functions have a clear input-output contract: they take Ontology objects or text strings as input and return objects/strings or make Ontology edits.
Ontology edits from Logic functions can be automated with human-in-the-loop review, allowing staged approval before changes are applied.
The block-based composition model chains Ontology reads, writes, calculations, aggregations, and LLM interactions into coherent workflows.
AIP Logic shares the same rigorous security model as the rest of Palantir, including user-level and function-level permissions controlling LLM data access.

title: AIP Agent Studio - Overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: A development environment for building interactive AI assistants (AIP Agents) equipped with enterprise-specific information and tools, deployable internally and externally via OSDK and platform APIs.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent
extractedEntities.description: An interactive assistant built in Agent Studio that is powered by LLMs, the Ontology, documents, and custom tools, enabling dynamic read and write workflows.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Threads
extractedEntities.description: An ad-hoc LLM interaction interface for document analysis and productivity, serving as the entry point (Tier 1) in the agent tier framework.

extractedEntities.name: OSDK
extractedEntities.description: The Ontology Software Development Kit used to deploy AIP Agents externally in third-party applications through platform APIs.

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's application development platform where AIP Agents can be incorporated using the AIP Agent widget for agentic applications.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Automate
extractedEntities.description: An automation platform where published agent functions can be pulled in to enable agents to handle complex workflows autonomously.

extractedEntities.name: Agent Tier Framework
extractedEntities.description: A four-tier progression for building AIP agents: Tier 1 (ad-hoc analysis), Tier 2 (task-specific agent), Tier 3 (agentic application), Tier 4 (automated agent).

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that provide structured context for AIP Agents and can be read or written through agent interactions.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology operations that AIP Agents can execute to write data and perform operations as part of dynamic workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology-backed logic that agents can call, including published AIP Logic functions, enabling complex tool-based reasoning.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Ontology abstraction layer that agents can use to interact with Object Types in a polymorphic manner.

extractedEntities.name: Application Variable
extractedEntities.description: A Workshop state variable that AIP Agents can read from and update, enabling bidirectional communication between agents and the application UI.

keyInsights: AIP Agent Studio provides a four-tier progression framework: ad-hoc analysis (Threads), task-specific agents, agentic applications (Workshop), and fully automated agents (Automate).
Agents can be deployed both internally within the Palantir platform and externally through the OSDK and platform APIs, enabling enterprise-wide and customer-facing AI assistants.
Application variables enable bidirectional state sharing between AIP Agents and Workshop applications, allowing agents to both read and update application context.
The tier framework enables progressive complexity: starting from simple document Q&A to fully autonomous agents that handle complex workflows without human interaction.
AIP Agents are powered by four context sources: LLMs, the Ontology (objects, actions, functions), documents, and custom tools, creating rich enterprise-grounded reasoning.

title: AIP Agent Studio - Tools - Overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Action Tool
extractedEntities.description: An Agent Studio tool that gives agents the ability to execute Ontology edits, configurable for automatic execution or user confirmation before running.

extractedEntities.name: Object Query Tool
extractedEntities.description: An Agent Studio tool that specifies which Object Types an LLM can access, supporting filtering, aggregation, inspection, and link traversal with configurable property visibility for token efficiency.

extractedEntities.name: Function Tool
extractedEntities.description: An Agent Studio tool that allows LLMs to call any Foundry function including published AIP Logic functions, with version control support.

extractedEntities.name: Command Tool
extractedEntities.description: An Agent Studio tool that enables agents to trigger operations in other Palantir applications using commands, acting on behalf of users.

extractedEntities.name: Request Clarification Tool
extractedEntities.description: An Agent Studio tool that allows agents to pause execution and request clarification from users before proceeding.

extractedEntities.name: Native Tool Calling
extractedEntities.description: A tool mode that uses built-in LLM capabilities to provide and call tools directly, enabling parallel tool calls with improved speed and token efficiency.

extractedEntities.name: Prompted Tool Calling
extractedEntities.description: A tool mode that inserts instructions into the prompt for tool usage, supporting all tool types and models but limited to sequential single-tool calls.

extractedEntities.name: Update Application Variable Tool
extractedEntities.description: An Agent Studio tool used to update the value of application variables configured in the application state tab.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: The development environment where these six tool types are configured and provided to AI agents.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Ontology abstraction that agents interact with through object query tools for polymorphic access to data.

keyInsights: Agent Studio provides six distinct tool types: action, object query, function, update application variable, command, and request clarification, each serving a specific capability.
Object query tools support filtering, aggregation, inspection, and link traversal with configurable property visibility, enabling token-efficient data access for LLMs.
Native tool calling enables parallel tool execution with improved speed and token efficiency, but is limited to specific models and tool types (actions, object query, function, update variable).
Prompted tool calling provides universal compatibility across all models and tool types but is limited to sequential single-tool calls per turn.
Action tools can be configured for automatic execution or user confirmation, providing granular control over the level of human oversight in agentic workflows.
The View Reasoning feature allows inspection of the LLM's decision-making process for each response, supporting transparency and debugging.

title: AIP Evals - Overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: A testing environment specifically designed to evaluate the performance of LLM-powered functions, handling non-deterministic behavior through test cases, evaluation functions, and cross-run comparison.

extractedEntities.name: Evaluation Suite
extractedEntities.description: The collection of test cases, target functions, and evaluation functions used to benchmark LLM-powered function performance in AIP Evals.

extractedEntities.name: Target Function
extractedEntities.description: The LLM-powered function being evaluated within an AIP Evals suite, with support for testing multiple target functions simultaneously.

extractedEntities.name: Evaluation Function
extractedEntities.description: A method for comparing or evaluating actual output against expected output, implementable as no-code, Python, TypeScript, or LLM-as-judge.

extractedEntities.name: Test Case
extractedEntities.description: Defined sets of inputs and expected outputs that are passed into evaluation functions during evaluation suite runs.

extractedEntities.name: Evaluation Metric
extractedEntities.description: Results of evaluation functions produced per test case, comparable in aggregate or individually between runs.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology concept that AIP Evals evaluates, including AIP Logic functions, AIP Agent functions, and code-authored functions.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: The no-code function development environment whose functions are primary targets for AIP Evals evaluation.

extractedEntities.name: LLM-as-Judge
extractedEntities.description: An evaluation pattern where an LLM is used as the evaluation function to assess the quality of another LLM's output.

extractedEntities.name: Grid Search Parameter Testing
extractedEntities.description: A technique in AIP Evals for systematically testing different parameter combinations to evaluate function performance across variations.

keyInsights: AIP Evals is purpose-built for the non-deterministic nature of LLMs, enabling variance examination across multiple runs and cross-version comparison.
Evaluation suites support simultaneous testing of multiple target functions, enabling side-by-side model comparison on identical test cases.
Four evaluator authoring modes are available: no-code, Python, TypeScript, and LLM-as-judge, accommodating different skill levels and evaluation needs.
Metrics are produced per test case and can be compared in aggregate or individually between runs, supporting both macro and micro performance analysis.
The system supports evaluating AIP Logic functions, AIP Agent functions, and code-authored functions within a unified evaluation framework.

title: Semantic search - Overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: A search capability that finds text based on inherent meaning and context using vector embeddings, rather than relying on keywords or traditional search methods.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Embedding
extractedEntities.description: Arrays of numbers produced by AI models that represent text as points in N-dimensional space, where proximity indicates semantic similarity.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that can be associated with embedded text, making search-driven operational workflows contextually grounded in structured data.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Typed attributes of Object Types, including vector properties that store embeddings for semantic search capabilities.

extractedEntities.name: KNN Search
extractedEntities.description: K-nearest-neighbors search that finds the nearest vectors in N-dimensional space to identify semantically related entities.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform that hosts the Ontology and provides the infrastructure for semantic search workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Document Chunking
extractedEntities.description: A technique for splitting documents into smaller pieces before embedding, improving semantic search relevance for long-form content.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Property
extractedEntities.description: A special Property type on Object Types that stores embedding vectors, enabling semantic similarity search against Ontology objects.

keyInsights: Semantic search uses AI models to transform text into vector embeddings where proximity in N-dimensional space indicates semantic similarity, enabling meaning-based retrieval.
Associating embedded text with Ontology objects creates operationally grounded search: finding related entities becomes a matter of finding nearest vectors in embedding space.
Vector properties on Object Types are the bridge between unstructured text and structured Ontology data, enabling hybrid structured-semantic queries.
Palantir supports both platform-provided and custom embedding models, and provides document chunking workflows for improving search quality on long-form content.
Semantic search is a foundational capability for OAG, providing the retrieval mechanism that surfaces relevant Ontology context for LLM reasoning.

title: AIP architecture overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform for connecting generative AI into operational domains, structured across 12 capability categories spanning secure LLM integration, context engineering, agents, evaluation, and developer toolchain.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform that operates together with AIP as part of a shared service mesh, providing the data infrastructure and Ontology system.

extractedEntities.name: Apollo
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's deployment and infrastructure management platform that powers AIP and Foundry within the Rubix deployment environment.

extractedEntities.name: Rubix
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's deployment environment within which Apollo deploys and manages AIP and Foundry services.

extractedEntities.name: Durable Orchestration
extractedEntities.description: AIP's execution model for pipeline-style AI workflows that can be configured through low-code (AIP Logic) or pro-code (Code Workspaces) interfaces.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology System
extractedEntities.description: The central system that activates context by integrating data, logic, action, and security into a unified representation of enterprise decision-making, with a language, engine, and toolchain.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry tool for data integration and transformation workflows used in context engineering for AIP.

extractedEntities.name: Code Workspaces
extractedEntities.description: Pro-code development environments in Foundry for building custom functions, models, and orchestrations.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Language
extractedEntities.description: The Ontology's modeling system that represents the nouns and verbs of operational processes into a legible form for both humans and AI agents.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Engine
extractedEntities.description: The execution layer enabling querying of billions of objects, orchestrating tens of thousands of actions, and incorporating feedback-based learning.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Toolchain
extractedEntities.description: The developer-facing layer empowering builders to create diverse AI-powered applications atop the common Ontology foundation.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Ontology abstraction referenced in the architecture for enabling polymorphic access across the platform.

extractedEntities.name: Context Engineering
extractedEntities.description: The practice of equipping developers with no-, low-, and pro-code tools for integrating contextual data, logic, and action that power the Ontology and dependent workflows.

keyInsights: The AIP architecture is organized into 12 capability categories: secure LLM integration, observability, context engineering, Ontology system, vector/compute/tool services, security, agents, evaluation, automation, developer toolchain, model management, and deployment.

The Ontology system has three distinct layers: a language (modeling nouns and verbs), an engine (querying billions of objects and orchestrating actions), and a toolchain (developer-facing application building).
AIP and Foundry operate as a shared service mesh powered by Apollo and deployed within Rubix, forming a tightly integrated but conceptually distinct platform stack.

Context engineering spans no-code, low-code, and pro-code tools, supporting all data integration modalities (batch, streaming, real-time CDC) across multiple runtimes (Spark, Flink, DataFusion, Polars).
Durable orchestrations enable pipeline-style AI workflows that can recover from failures and maintain state, configurable through either AIP Logic or Code Workspaces.
The platform is designed to be modular and extensible with respect to models, compute engines, and interfaces, supporting the full range of commercial and open-source LLMs.

title: Palantir MCP - Overview - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Palantir MCP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's implementation of the Model Context Protocol that enables AI IDEs and agents to autonomously design, build, edit, and review applications within the Palantir platform.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology MCP
extractedEntities.description: A consumption-focused MCP that enables external AI systems to safely read and write Ontology data through application-scoped object types, action types, and query functions exposed as MCP tools.

extractedEntities.name: OSDK
extractedEntities.description: The Ontology Software Development Kit that Palantir MCP provides context for, including OSDK-specific code examples and repository detection.

extractedEntities.name: Developer Console
extractedEntities.description: A Palantir tool for managing applications and OSDK configurations, which Palantir MCP can search and update.

extractedEntities.name: Model Context Protocol
extractedEntities.description: An open protocol standard for providing AI systems with context and tools, which Palantir implements for platform-wide AI IDE integration.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that both Palantir MCP and Ontology MCP expose to external AI systems for querying and data operations.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology operations exposed through Ontology MCP as tools that external AI agents can execute within application scope restrictions.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology query functions exposed through Ontology MCP that external systems can invoke for data retrieval.

extractedEntities.name: Copilot Studio
extractedEntities.description: A Microsoft product that can connect to Ontology MCP to execute Palantir actions and write data through external AI agents.

extractedEntities.name: Gemini Enterprise
extractedEntities.description: A Google product that can connect to Ontology MCP for reading and writing Ontology data through external AI systems.

extractedEntities.name: Code Snippet Index
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's searchable index of code examples that MCP uses to provide context for libraries that do not fit specific repository types.

keyInsights: Palantir offers two distinct MCP implementations: Palantir MCP for ontology builders (can modify types but not data) and Ontology MCP for consumers (can safely read/write data but not modify types).
Palantir MCP provides both context (internal library documentation, Foundry architecture knowledge) and tools (ontology exploration, project modification) to external AI agents.
Ontology MCP restricts agent actions through application scopes, ensuring external AI systems can only execute operations within predefined boundaries.
The MCP implementation auto-detects repository type (OSDK, Python transforms, TypeScript functions) and injects tailored context for the detected type.
External AI systems like Copilot Studio and Gemini Enterprise can natively integrate with Palantir's Ontology through the MCP standard, enabling cross-platform AI workflows.
Palantir MCP can autonomously design, build, edit, and review applications covering data integration, ontology configuration, and application development.

title: AI Infrastructure & Ontology | Palantir & NVIDIA - Palantir Blog

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform through which NVIDIA models and accelerated computing capabilities are made available to enterprise customers.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform that integrates NVIDIA accelerated computing and models for enterprise AI workflows.

extractedEntities.name: NVIDIA Nemotron Super
extractedEntities.description: NVIDIA's state-of-the-art 49B parameter agentic reasoning model built using Llama, available through Palantir's Model Catalog on secure cloud infrastructure.

extractedEntities.name: NVIDIA NeMo Retriever
extractedEntities.description: NVIDIA's state-of-the-art embedding model for RAG and OAG workflows, available via Pipeline Builder, Code Workspaces, and the Ontology Toolchain.

extractedEntities.name: NVIDIA cuOpt
extractedEntities.description: NVIDIA's optimization engine supporting linear programming and mixed integer linear programming for supply chain challenges like inventory rebalancing and vehicle routing.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry tool that can leverage NVIDIA NeMo Retriever for building embedding-based RAG and OAG workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Model Catalog
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's model registry where NVIDIA Nemotron and other models are made available to platform users.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that are central to the operational workflows accelerated by NVIDIA compute infrastructure.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology logic assets, including agentic functions built through AIP Logic, that benefit from NVIDIA accelerated compute.

extractedEntities.name: Supply Chain Optimization
extractedEntities.description: A Lowe's use case moving from weekly single-node optimization to continuous dynamic global optimization using the combined NVIDIA-Palantir stack.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: RAG workflows enhanced by structured Ontology context, improved by NVIDIA NeMo Retriever embeddings for better semantic grounding.

keyInsights: The NVIDIA partnership integrates three capabilities: Nemotron Super (agentic reasoning), NeMo Retriever (embeddings for RAG/OAG), and cuOpt (optimization), all accessible through the Ontology.
NVIDIA models run on Palantir's secure cloud infrastructure with a guarantee that no transmitted data is retained by third-party providers or used for retraining.
The integration enables pushing the Ontology to the edge through NVIDIA's accelerated compute, extending AI-driven decision-making beyond centralized cloud environments.
Lowe's exemplifies the partnership's impact: moving from weekly single-node supply chain optimization to continuous dynamic optimization at global scale.
NVIDIA NeMo Retriever is available through multiple Foundry tools (Pipeline Builder, Code Workspaces, Ontology Toolchain), enabling embedding-based workflows across development modalities.

title: Purpose-based Access Controls at Palantir (Palantir Explained, #2)

extractedEntities.name: Purpose-Based Access Control
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry access control mechanism where users apply for access to a Purpose (a scoped collection of data for a specific goal) rather than individual datasets, with mandatory rationale recording for all access decisions.

extractedEntities.name: Purpose
extractedEntities.description: A data governance construct that scopes datasets to a specific user goal, ensuring users receive access to exactly the data needed for their purpose -- no more, no less.

extractedEntities.name: Access Rationale
extractedEntities.description: A mandatory justification recorded when granting access, capturing why a user was given access to data and why data was assigned to a Purpose, enabling audit transparency.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform that implements Purpose-Based Access Controls as a core governance mechanism integrated into the underlying access control system.

extractedEntities.name: Data Governance
extractedEntities.description: The organizational process of managing who has access to what data and why, which PBAC structures through Purposes and mandatory rationale recording.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities whose underlying datasets are governed through Purpose-Based Access Controls.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology operations whose data access is governed by Purpose-based scope.

extractedEntities.name: Audit Trail
extractedEntities.description: The complete record of access decisions including who has access, what data they can see, and the rationale for each decision, available to auditors at any point.

extractedEntities.name: COVID-19 Healthcare Data Governance
extractedEntities.description: A use case where healthcare organizations needed to rapidly bring together data from testing, care homes, and hospitals while maintaining strict access controls for thousands of users.

keyInsights: PBAC inverts the traditional access model: instead of requesting access to individual datasets, users apply for access to a Purpose that contains only the data scoped for their specific goal.
Mandatory rationale recording at both the user-access and data-scoping level creates a complete audit chain explaining not just who has access, but why they were given it.
PBAC is designed for non-technical data governance teams, providing intuitive tooling to enforce access rules without relying on technical colleagues.
The system scales with organizational complexity: as datasets and users grow, Purposes prevent exponential growth of individual access decisions.
PBAC addresses the fundamental governance question of necessity and proportionality -- data governance teams must continually justify that access is strictly necessary for the stated purpose.

title: Overview - Security - Palantir Foundry Documentation

extractedEntities.name: Mandatory Controls
extractedEntities.description: Security controls that propagate along with each unit of data or resource type via Palantir's provenance and lineage capabilities, including Organizations and markings.

extractedEntities.name: Discretionary Controls
extractedEntities.description: Permissions granted to users on individual resources in the form of roles with different operations (view, edit), providing flexible granular access control.

extractedEntities.name: Role
extractedEntities.description: A named set of permissions (e.g., Viewer, Editor) that can be assigned to users on resources, forming the basis of discretionary access control in Foundry.

extractedEntities.name: Organization
extractedEntities.description: A form of mandatory control applied to Projects that enforces strict silos between groups of users and resources in the platform.

extractedEntities.name: Marking
extractedEntities.description: A mandatory control that propagates through data lineage, used to tag sensitive information and enforce access restrictions that follow the data as it moves through the platform.

extractedEntities.name: Row-Level Security
extractedEntities.description: Granular access controls based on user attributes that restrict which rows of data within a resource a user can access.

extractedEntities.name: Column-Level Security
extractedEntities.description: Granular access controls based on user attributes that restrict which columns (properties) within a resource a user can see.

extractedEntities.name: Project
extractedEntities.description: The organizational unit in Palantir where data and resources are organized, forming the basis for both mandatory (Organization) and discretionary (role-based) access control.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform with security as a core development philosophy, implementing strict enforcement, granular controls, transparency, and usability.

extractedEntities.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
extractedEntities.description: Mandatory authentication requirement for all Palantir managed SaaS platform customers, part of the high minimum security bar.

extractedEntities.name: Provenance and Lineage
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's capability to track the origin and transformation path of data, used as the propagation mechanism for mandatory security controls.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology property that can be shared across Object Types, requiring its own security consideration in the platform's permission model.

keyInsights: Palantir's security model combines mandatory controls (propagating via data lineage) and discretionary controls (role-based, per-resource), creating a layered defense model.
Mandatory controls follow data as it moves through the platform via provenance and lineage tracking, ensuring security is never lost through data transformations.
The platform supports granular row-level and column-level security based on user attributes, enabling fine-grained data access within shared resources.
Projects serve as the fundamental organizational unit for security, with data, resources, users, and groups all organized and permissioned through Projects.
Organizations enforce strict silos between groups of users and resources as a form of mandatory control, preventing cross-organizational data leakage.
Multi-factor authentication is mandatory (not optional) for all managed SaaS customers, reflecting Palantir's opinionated high-security-minimum approach.

title: Concepts - Classification-based Access Controls - Palantir Foundry Documentation

extractedEntities.name: Classification-Based Access Control
extractedEntities.description: Mandatory controls for protecting sensitive government information using hierarchical classification markings (e.g., Secret, Top Secret) that restrict access based on a user's clearance level.

extractedEntities.name: Classification Marking
extractedEntities.description: A security label from a configured category that restricts access hierarchically, supporting both conjunctive (AND) and disjunctive (OR) behavior across categories.

extractedEntities.name: Marking Category
extractedEntities.description: A grouping of classification markings that defines either the type of data or how it should be disseminated, configured with conjunctive or disjunctive behavior.

extractedEntities.name: Hierarchical Access
extractedEntities.description: A classification model where higher clearance levels automatically include access to lower levels (e.g., Top Secret users can access Secret data).

extractedEntities.name: Disjunctive Marking
extractedEntities.description: A classification marking behavior where satisfying membership in any one of multiple groups (OR logic) grants access, commonly used for releasability between organizations or countries.

extractedEntities.name: Conjunctive Marking
extractedEntities.description: A classification marking behavior where a user must have all markings from a category (AND logic) to access classified data.

extractedEntities.name: Project Classification
extractedEntities.description: A required classification setting on Projects in CBAC-enabled environments that ensures all organizational containers have an explicit security classification.

extractedEntities.name: Data Classification
extractedEntities.description: A required classification on all datasets in CBAC-enabled environments, with raw datasets requiring explicit file classification.

extractedEntities.name: Project
extractedEntities.description: The organizational unit that is required to have a Project Classification set in CBAC-enabled environments.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform that supports CBAC as an optional mandatory control layer, requiring Palantir involvement for configuration.

keyInsights: CBAC provides hierarchical security clearance modeling: Top Secret access automatically includes Secret and below, matching real-world government classification structures.
Classification markings support both conjunctive (must have ALL markings) and disjunctive (must have ANY ONE marking) behavior, enabling complex multi-national releasability rules.
In CBAC-enabled environments, classifications are ubiquitous: all Projects must have a Project Classification and all datasets must have a Data Classification, leaving no gaps.
CBAC markings are mandatory controls that combine with other access requirements (discretionary roles, mandatory markings), creating a multi-layered security model.
Configuration of classification markings requires Palantir involvement, reflecting the high-stakes nature of government classification and the need for correct initial setup.

title: Object permissioning - Ontology permissions - Palantir Foundry Documentation

extractedEntities.name: Project-Based Permissions
extractedEntities.description: A unified permission model where Ontology resources (object types, action types, link types, interfaces, shared properties) inherit permissions from their containing Compass project.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Permissions
extractedEntities.description: The central permissioning model for Ontology resources, managed through Compass (the platform filesystem), replacing legacy ontology roles and datasource-derived permissions.

extractedEntities.name: Compass
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform filesystem that manages permissions for all resource types including Ontology resources, providing a unified permission interface.

extractedEntities.name: Role
extractedEntities.description: A set of permissions (Viewer, Editor, Owner) assigned to users on Compass projects that determine their access to contained Ontology resources.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology resource that is now saved as a file in a Compass project, with view/edit/manage permissions determined by the user's project role.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology resource requiring edit permissions on both the link type itself and the linked object types for modification.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology resource requiring edit permissions on both the action type and all ontology resource types it edits.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology abstraction that inherits permissions from its containing Compass project under the project-based permissions model.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology property shared across Object Types that inherits permissions from its containing Compass project.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type vs Object Permissions
extractedEntities.description: A key distinction: viewing an Object Type (schema) requires View on the object type only, but viewing actual Objects (data) requires View on both the object type AND the backing datasource.

extractedEntities.name: Datasource Permissions
extractedEntities.description: Separate permissions on the backing data store of an Object Type, required in addition to Object Type permissions to view actual object instances.

keyInsights: Object Type permissions and Object permissions are fundamentally different: viewing schema requires only Object Type access, but viewing actual data requires both Object Type AND datasource access.
Project-based permissions unify Ontology resource permissioning with the rest of the platform through Compass, eliminating the need for separate ontology-specific permission systems.
Link type edits require permissions on both the link type and all linked object types, while action type edits require permissions on the action type and all affected resource types.
The new model replaces two legacy systems (ontology roles and datasource-derived permissions) with a single unified approach based on Compass projects.
Ontology resources gain access to Compass features like folders, access requests, markings, and tags by becoming project-based resources, enriching governance capabilities.
Bulk permission management is enabled by setting permissions at the folder or project level, eliminating the need to permission individual ontology items.
